external_accountability.findings: An article reports on the FCA and MAS collaboration to promote safe AI innovation, implying external oversight. The article also references IOSCO and RegTech, supporting the presence of external accountability policies for AI risks.

relevant_sources.summary: This article provides evidence for **external_accountability**, **governance**, and **transparency** by reporting on the FCA and MAS collaboration to promote safe AI innovation, which implies external oversight and governance. It also highlights Interactive Brokers' engagement with AI-driven tools, suggesting a focus on governance and transparency in their deployment for investors. The mention of IOSCO and RegTech further supports the presence of external accountability and governance policies for AI risks.
relevant_sources.title: FCA and MAS Collaboration on AI Begins Amid Growing Broker Integration

It also describes the capabilities of the "IBot Natural Language Trading Interface," such as generative AI for summarization and automated scanner functions. An article highlights the company's engagement with AI-driven tools, suggesting a focus on transparency in their deployment for investors.

summary.overall_findings: Based on 12 publicly available sources, Interactive Brokers' published materials address 4 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Transparency disclosures describe the AI capabilities of the "Ask IBKR AI-Powered Portfolio Analysis Tool," including intelligent question completion and suggested queries, while privacy policies detail data collection practices such as the use of cookies and profiling for targeted advertising. Further, governance materials reference proprietary techniques used in the development of the "Ask IBKR AI-Powered Portfolio Analysis Tool" and mention a processing technology and vendor for the "IBot Natural Language Trading Interface." External accountability is also addressed through an article reporting on the FCA and MAS collaboration to promote safe AI innovation. No qualifying public evidence was found for fairness, explainability, and 1 additional pillar.
